jokes.text: Girl, you know I ain't into that frat stuff. It's for a bunch of pocket-protector-wearing, no-social-life-having losers.
jokes.setup: Asked which fraternity house he's joining
jokes.punchline: Dismisses fraternities with specific nerdy stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Sets up Will's anti-fraternity stance with character-appropriate street smart dismissal

jokes.text: The defense rests, Your Honor.
jokes.setup: Will just criticized fraternities
jokes.punchline: Carlton responds with formal legal language like he's in court
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - overly formal and pretentious in typical character fashion

jokes.text: We're Delta Kappa Tau We'd like to take a bow / We got the highest grades And we will tell you how / We study, study, study For professional careers / We drink a lot of Ovaltine But never any beers
jokes.setup: Fraternity presentation expected to be cool
jokes.punchline: Nerdy rap about studying and drinking Ovaltine instead of beer
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy gold - the contrast between rap format and nerdy content is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Revenge of the Nerds: Part Six.
jokes.setup: Just witnessed the nerdy fraternity rap
jokes.punchline: References the movie franchise about nerds
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference that perfectly captures the moment - economical and effective

jokes.text: Where's, like, a big gong when you need one, you know?
jokes.setup: Lambda Kappa Nu performs their pretentious chant
jokes.punchline: References The Gong Show's hook for bad acts
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture callback that shows Will's street-smart perspective on pretentious behavior

jokes.text: Lambda Kappa Nu song about blue blood and gold cards
jokes.setup: Another fraternity trying to impress
jokes.punchline: Extremely elitist song about old money and privilege
jokes.explanation: Good contrast to the nerd fraternity - different kind of cringey privilege

jokes.text: Man! Hey, I guess that's a tough choice, huh, Carlton?
jokes.setup: Carlton just witnessed impressive Phi Beta Gamma step routine
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests Carlton has a difficult decision between cool Black fraternity and nerdy white ones
jokes.explanation: Will's sarcasm highlighting the obvious contrast between fraternity options

jokes.text: I'll say. Can I hear yours again?
jokes.setup: Will sarcastically says it's a tough choice
jokes.punchline: Carlton completely misses the sarcasm and wants to hear the cool step routine again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obliviousness - he's genuinely excited about the cool fraternity while missing Will's tone

jokes.text: Well, my gold card has a hologram of Lee Iacocca on it.
jokes.setup: Trying to impress the rich white fraternity members
jokes.punchline: Mentions having a business executive on his credit card like it's cool
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific Carlton line - only he would think Lee Iacocca is impressive to college kids

jokes.text: Excuse us, guys, it's time for a Negro moment.
jokes.setup: Need to talk privately about racial fraternity dynamics
jokes.punchline: Bluntly announces the racial subtext that's been building
jokes.explanation: Bold, direct acknowledgment of racial dynamics with Will's characteristic bluntness - cuts through pretense

jokes.text: I was. Didn't you notice we all had alligator shirts on?
jokes.setup: Will asks if he wants to join a fraternity where guys look more like him
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks Will means the preppy white guys because of their clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton misdirection - he identifies with prep style over race, missing Will's point entirely

jokes.text: Now, Top Dog, you know when it's me against you... it's one-on-none.
jokes.setup: Top Dog mentions missing their one-on-one basketball games
jokes.punchline: Twists 'one-on-one' to 'one-on-none' implying Top Dog has no game
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Will's quick wit and trash-talking skills in character

jokes.text: Are you in a fraternity? And here I thought you had a life.
jokes.setup: Top Dog reveals he's in a fraternity
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests fraternities are for people without lives
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to Will's earlier anti-fraternity stance, maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: But, I will have a double scoop of that, though.
jokes.setup: Rejects fraternity invitation
jokes.punchline: But is interested in the attractive women at the party
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's priorities - not interested in fraternity but definitely interested in meeting women

jokes.text: I wouldn't really have to go to juvenile hall, would I? I hear those kids are real troublemakers.
jokes.setup: Learning about fraternity's community service at juvenile hall
jokes.punchline: Carlton fears the 'troublemakers' despite being sheltered and privileged
jokes.explanation: Typical Carlton sheltered perspective - fears juvenile hall kids while being completely naive

jokes.text: Well, it don't get no littler than cousin Carlton.
jokes.setup: Top Dog says they need humor at the frat house
jokes.punchline: Will plays on 'little humor' to mock Carlton's size
jokes.explanation: Quick wordplay joke at Carlton's expense, typical cousin dynamic

jokes.text: Twenty-six years of marriage and I find you in bed with a cheerleader.
jokes.setup: Soap opera dramatic reveal
jokes.punchline: Cliché soap opera adultery plot
jokes.explanation: Generic soap opera parody setup - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: She really hasn't been the same since she had that sex change.
jokes.setup: Discussing soap opera character Mary
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions an extreme plot twist like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Absurd soap opera escalation delivered matter-of-factly - good surprise factor

jokes.text: I used to date a biker.
jokes.setup: Family discussing Philip's motorcycle
jokes.punchline: Hilary casually reveals unexpected past relationship
jokes.explanation: Brief character revelation that adds unexpected depth to ditzy Hilary

jokes.text: Maybe next time we can actually hit second gear.
jokes.setup: Philip excited about his motorcycle ride
jokes.punchline: Vivian sarcastically points out how slow they went
jokes.explanation: Vivian's dry wit deflating Philip's motorcycle enthusiasm

jokes.text: Looks like a bunch of middle-aged men with too much time on their hands.
jokes.setup: Philip claims they're bikers
jokes.punchline: Vivian calls out the midlife crisis reality
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observation humor about midlife crisis behavior

jokes.text: Dan, you're an accountant.
jokes.setup: Philip insists 'we're bikers'
jokes.punchline: Vivian deflates the fantasy with Dan's mundane reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation - economy of words with maximum impact

jokes.text: Got a moment, Fonzie?
jokes.setup: Philip on motorcycle trying to be cool
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey references Happy Days' Fonzie character
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that perfectly captures the absurdity of Philip's biker phase

jokes.text: Well, just look at it this way. If I take a fall, I have a 50-pound cushion.
jokes.setup: Vivian says motorcycles are dangerous
jokes.punchline: Philip jokes about his weight being protective
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that shows Philip's ability to laugh at himself

jokes.text: From what Helen tells me... she ain't giving him any heat anywhere else either.
jokes.setup: Philip compares their marriage to Dan and Helen's
jokes.punchline: Vivian reveals Dan's marriage problems with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Adult humor about marriage problems with decent setup and payoff

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I can't wait to see your first hot flash.
jokes.setup: Vivian tells him to get off the midlife crisis highway
jokes.punchline: Philip threatens to wait for her midlife crisis
jokes.explanation: Tit-for-tat marital sparring with age-related humor

jokes.text: Must be that midlife crisis thing Aunt Viv warned us about.
jokes.setup: Philip defensively refuses to discuss the motorcycle
jokes.punchline: Will directly identifies the midlife crisis
jokes.explanation: Obvious observation that connects to earlier scene - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Must be that midlife crisis thing Aunt Viv warned us about.
jokes.setup: Phil stubbornly defending his motorcycle purchase
jokes.punchline: Will referencing Vivian's earlier assessment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that ties the scenes together, shows Will paying attention

jokes.text: He's also an alcoholic. No, that's Judge Martin.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions Judge Morton is a Gamma
jokes.punchline: Philip confuses judges and their personal problems
jokes.explanation: Quick correction joke about mixing up professional colleagues

jokes.text: Oh, no. You know, Uncle Phil, I got a reputation more as a free spirit.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's pledging
jokes.punchline: Will describes his anti-establishment attitude as 'free spirit'
jokes.explanation: Will's diplomatic way of maintaining his anti-fraternity stance

jokes.text: The frat leader does need a little help with his jumping. Maybe I'll join as, like, a mercy thing.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil encouraging Will to join fraternity
jokes.punchline: Will condescends to help the leader with basketball
jokes.explanation: Classic Will arrogance - joining a fraternity as a favor to help them

jokes.text: Oh, but you better not try none of that run-out-of-gas stuff, mister.
jokes.setup: Philip offers to take Will for a motorcycle ride
jokes.punchline: Will warns against the classic excuse for romantic situations
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo joke about the classic 'running out of gas' pickup line

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah, just relax, Magellan.
jokes.setup: Fraternity leader being overly dramatic about 'the journey begins'
jokes.punchline: Will mocks the grandiosity by comparing him to the explorer
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of pretentious fraternity ceremony with historical reference

jokes.text: Well, are you speaking to me now, or are you?
jokes.setup: Told he only speaks when spoken to
jokes.punchline: Carlton creates logical paradox about the rule itself
jokes.explanation: Clever Carlton logic that exposes the absurdity of fraternity rules - well-constructed

jokes.text: Actually, my butler handles the laundry.
jokes.setup: Assigned to do laundry as punishment
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals his sheltered privilege to urban Black fraternity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obliviousness to social dynamics - shows cultural disconnect

jokes.text: South central Bel-Air.
jokes.setup: Asked where he's from, says Bel-Air
jokes.punchline: Tries to make Bel-Air sound urban by adding 'south central'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Carlton attempt to sound street - combines wealthy area with dangerous neighborhood designation

jokes.text: I live right across the street from the Aaron Spelling mansion. You know, the producer of Beverly Hills, 90210?
jokes.setup: Trying to impress the fraternity brothers
jokes.punchline: References TV producer like it makes him cool to urban audience
jokes.explanation: Carlton's continued cluelessness about what impresses street-smart fraternity members

jokes.text: Dylan's on the verge of getting back with Brenda. They really getting back together, though?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions 90210, Top Dog dismisses it
jokes.punchline: Top Dog secretly wants to know about the soap opera plot
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - tough fraternity leader caught caring about teen soap opera

jokes.text: They really getting back together, though?
jokes.setup: Carlton's enthusiasm about TV romance
jokes.punchline: Top Dog gets sucked into the drama despite himself
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing even the cool guy gets caught up in soap opera

jokes.text: No need to thank him all at once, fellas.
jokes.setup: Carlton gets everyone in trouble with extra work
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests the other pledges are grateful
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic reaction to Carlton causing group punishment

jokes.text: Brother from another planet.
jokes.setup: Discussing Carlton's odd behavior
jokes.punchline: References sci-fi movie to describe Carlton's alienness
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that captures Carlton's outsider status

jokes.text: Hey, we're gonna play Hollywood Squares.
jokes.setup: Being split into groups for some fraternity activity
jokes.punchline: Will references game show based on the square formation
jokes.explanation: Simple visual observation comparing fraternity formation to game show

jokes.text: Yeah, but the rest of us got to wear shoes.
jokes.setup: Carlton says everyone had to help re-tar the parking lot
jokes.punchline: Will points out Carlton was singled out for worse treatment
jokes.explanation: Simple observation that highlights the unfair treatment Carlton is receiving

jokes.text: I had no idea how much Mary was suffering.
jokes.setup: Soap opera continues with Mary and Lazlo drama
jokes.punchline: Ashley emotionally invested in fictional character's pain
jokes.explanation: Continues the soap opera addiction theme - functional callback

jokes.text: Motorcycles and black leather will never make me feel as young... as the love of a good woman.
jokes.setup: Philip claims to have come to his senses about the motorcycle
jokes.punchline: Delivers cheesy romantic speech about Vivian
jokes.explanation: Clichéd romantic speech that feels like Philip overcompensating

jokes.text: Oh, sir, the body shop called. They said your motorcycle is completely totaled.
jokes.setup: Philip just gave romantic speech about choosing love over motorcycles
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals the real reason Philip gave up the bike
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic timing that completely undercuts Philip's romantic gesture - excellent misdirection

jokes.text: but have to pay for that box of Eskimo Pies you ate.
jokes.setup: Already revealed Philip crashed into ice cream truck
jokes.punchline: Adds embarrassing detail about Philip eating the evidence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that adds to Philip's embarrassment with character-appropriate gluttony

jokes.text: What would that be, One Life To Ruin?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentioning his favorite soap opera
jokes.punchline: Phil's bitter pun on 'One Life to Live'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that captures Phil's frustration at having his life exposed

jokes.text: What would that be, One Life To Ruin?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentions his favorite soap opera
jokes.punchline: Philip plays on soap opera titles with Geoffrey's destructive timing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that references Geoffrey's tendency to ruin Philip's moments

jokes.text: I broke my bike.
jokes.setup: After elaborate story about choosing love over motorcycles
jokes.punchline: Philip admits the simple truth like a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect childlike admission that strips away all pretense - excellent economy and character work

jokes.text: You've never said that to me before in my life. Give me a hug. Don't.
jokes.setup: Will says he's proud of Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton gets emotional, Will immediately shuts it down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will-Carlton dynamic - rare vulnerability immediately shut down by Will's discomfort

jokes.text: You see, man, that is exactly why I'll never tell you nothing.
jokes.setup: Carlton wants a hug after Will's rare praise
jokes.punchline: Will explains why he avoids being nice to Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the Will-Carlton relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Girl, if God made anything prettier than you... I hope he kept it for himself.
jokes.setup: Will approaching attractive woman at party
jokes.punchline: Delivers smooth pickup line with religious twist
jokes.explanation: Classic Will smooth-talking with creative religious angle on compliment

jokes.text: Well then, mush, you huskies.
jokes.setup: Being told about attractive women being 'tip of the iceberg'
jokes.punchline: Will plays with ice/cold metaphor by referencing dog sledding
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay following the ice metaphor to its absurd conclusion

jokes.text: The last time we played them, the party just kind of got out of hand. I know what you mean. You should see what happens at their concerts.
jokes.setup: Top Dog sarcastically says Rod Stewart/Neil Diamond got party out of hand
jokes.punchline: Carlton completely misses the sarcasm and agrees seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obliviousness - thinks middle-aged crooners cause wild parties

jokes.text: Well, Carlton is not like you and me. Carlton doesn't exactly exemplify what I think a Phi Beta Gamma is.
jokes.setup: Will asks what Top Dog means by Carlton not being 'their type'
jokes.punchline: Top Dog reveals racial/cultural prejudice against Carlton
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable setup for the episode's serious racial commentary

jokes.text: Everything your butler does for you.
jokes.setup: Top Dog listing Carlton's menial tasks during pledging
jokes.punchline: Reference back to Carlton's earlier butler admission
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Top Dog was paying attention and using Carlton's privilege against him

jokes.text: I'm not accepting no prep school, Bel-Air bred sellout into my fraternity.
jokes.setup: Carlton's confusion about rejection
jokes.punchline: Top Dog's harsh judgment of Carlton's background
jokes.explanation: Sets up the serious racial discussion - not primarily comedic but necessary

jokes.text: Or maybe it's because I like Barry Manilow.
jokes.setup: Carlton defending himself against sellout accusations
jokes.punchline: Carlton's admission of his most uncool musical taste
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where Carlton owns his uncool tastes during his serious speech

jokes.text: He mean Barry White, y'all.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions liking Barry Manilow
jokes.punchline: Fraternity member tries to save Carlton by suggesting he meant Barry White
jokes.explanation: Attempt to help Carlton by suggesting more culturally appropriate musical preference

jokes.text: And in the words of my illustrious cousin: 'We will make like a tree and leave.'
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to deliver cool exit line
jokes.punchline: Mangles the common expression
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton attempt to be cool that backfires through malapropism

jokes.text: I never say that. It's 'make like a banana and split.'
jokes.setup: Carlton attributes mangled expression to Will
jokes.punchline: Will corrects the saying while being annoyed at misattribution
jokes.explanation: Will's exasperated correction of Carlton's mangled expression

jokes.text: No. Apparently, I'm not enough of a brother to be a brother.
jokes.setup: Asked if they're the newest Phi Beta Gammas
jokes.punchline: Carlton plays on 'brother' meaning both family and fraternity member
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that captures the pain of racial rejection with perfect economy

jokes.text: Well, who does? I mean, you wait and wait... and when you finally get to the front of the line, everything's gone.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions not liking sellouts
jokes.punchline: Philip thinks Carlton means retail sellouts, not racial sellouts
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Philip's complete misunderstanding of racial terminology

jokes.text: The Jamma Gammas. The bad mama jamma. The bad mama jammas, yeah.
jokes.setup: Outtakes of Carlton struggling with fraternity name
jokes.punchline: Multiple attempts at saying 'Phi Beta Gamma' all wrong
jokes.explanation: Blooper reel material showing Carlton's continued struggles with street terminology
